label: Strait of Hormuz blockade drives global oil prices above one hundred dollars
label_zh: 荷姆茲海峽封鎖推高全球油價至每桶一百美元以上

timeline.title: Global Oil Prices Near Record Highs as Iran Attacks Gulf Energy Facilities Following Israeli Strikes
timeline.title_zh: 伊朗襲擊海灣能源設施後，全球油價逼近歷史高點
timeline.sub_title: Brent crude surged toward $120 on Thursday as Tehran launched retaliatory strikes against Gulf energy infrastructure, deepening the global crisis triggered by the ongoing blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 週四，布蘭特原油飆升至 120 美元附近，因德黑蘭對海灣能源基礎設施發動報復性襲擊，加劇了荷姆茲海峽持續封鎖引發的全球危機。
timeline.summary:
The global energy crisis took a sharp turn for the worse on Thursday as Brent crude oil prices surged above $119 per barrel, approaching an all-time high. This latest spike follows a series of dramatic military escalations in the Persian Gulf, where Iranian forces launched a wave of retaliatory drone and missile attacks against critical energy infrastructure in neighboring states. [ref:9] The attacks targeted the world's largest liquefied natural gas complex in Qatar, alongside refineries and gas units in Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the United Arab Emirates. [ref:8] These strikes were a direct response to a precision Israeli operation on Wednesday that severely damaged Iran’s South Pars gas field facilities, the largest of their kind in the world.

While global benchmarks like Brent and West Texas Intermediate showed significant volatility, physical crude markets are experiencing even more extreme stress. In regions outside the immediate blockade zone, such as Oman, physical barrels of crude reached a staggering $154 per barrel as buyers scrambled for non-Gulf supplies. Experts [ref:11] noted that the de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz has now reduced maritime traffic by 97%, effectively severing the world’s primary energy artery. With nearly [ref:7] 20 million barrels of oil and significant volumes of liquefied natural gas trapped behind the Iranian-enforced blockade, the global economy is facing what the International Energy Agency has described as the largest supply disruption in history.

On the diplomatic front, the International Maritime Organization convened an extraordinary session in London to address the growing threat to international shipping. A coalition of seven nations—including the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and Japan—issued a joint statement condemning Iran’s weaponization of international shipping and demanding an immediate cessation of hostilities. However, significant strategic friction remains within the Western alliance. Several European and NATO partners have expressed reluctance to join a U.S.-led military effort to reopen the waterway, citing the lack of a UN mandate and fears of being drawn into a wider ground war. In contrast, U [ref:2].S. military officials stated that the pressure is intensifying, with American aircraft penetrating deeper into Iranian airspace to target naval assets and mine storage facilities.

The domestic impact of the crisis is becoming increasingly visible across the globe. In the United States, the national average for regular gasoline climbed to $3.88 per gallon, a two-year high that reflects the immediate pass-through of rising crude costs. To mitigate the [ref:4] shock, the U.S. Treasury announced that the administration is considering an additional release from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, following a historic 400-million-barrel release authorized last week. Experts warn that these measures may only provide temporary relief as long as the primary transit route remains impassable.

As the conflict enters its third week, Iranian officials have signaled that the status of the Strait of Hormuz will not return to pre-war conditions. Lawmakers in Tehran are reportedly drafting a bill to impose transit tolls and security taxes on any future commercial traffic through the waterway. Economists warn that [ref:6] if the blockade persists through the end of the month, the sustained high prices could trigger a $500 billion shock to the global economy, potentially cutting global growth by half a percentage point in the fourth quarter.
timeline.summary_zh:
週四，全球能源危機急轉直下，布蘭特原油價格飆升至每桶 119 美元以上，逼近歷史最高紀錄。此最新飆升發生在一系列波斯灣戲劇性軍事升級之後，伊朗軍隊對鄰國的關鍵能源基礎設施發動了一系列報復性無人機和導彈襲擊。襲擊目標是卡塔爾擁有世界最大的液化天然氣綜合設施，以及沙烏地阿拉伯、科威特和阿拉伯聯合大公國的煉油廠和燃氣裝置。這些襲擊是對以色列週三一次精確行動的直接回應，該行動嚴重損壞了伊朗南帕斯氣田設施，這是世界上最大的同類設施。

儘管布蘭特和西德州輕原油等全球基準油價顯示出顯著波動，但實體原油市場正經歷更極端的壓力。在波斯灣封鎖區之外的地區，例如阿曼，實體原油每桶價格高達 154 美元，買家紛紛搶購非海灣供應。專家指出，荷姆茲海峽的實際關閉已將海上交通量減少了 97%，有效地切斷了世界主要的能源動脈。隨著近 2000 萬桶石油和大量液化天然氣被困在伊朗實施的封鎖後方，全球經濟正麵臨國際能源署 (IEA) 所稱的歷史上最大供應中斷。

外交方面，國際海事組織在倫敦召開了一次特別會議，以應對日益增長的國際航運威脅。由七個國家組成的聯盟——包括美國、加拿大、英國和日本——發表聯合聲明，譴責伊朗將國際航運武器化，並要求立即停止敵對行動。然而，西方聯盟內部仍然存在顯著的戰略摩擦。一些歐洲和北約夥伴對加入美國領導的重開水道軍事行動表現出猶豫，理由是缺乏聯合國授權以及擔心被捲入一場更大範圍的地面戰爭。相比之下，美國軍方官員表示，壓力正在加劇，美國飛機深入伊朗領空，目標是海軍資產和地雷儲存設施。

危機的國內影響在全球範圍內越來越明顯。在美國，普通汽油的全國平均價格上漲到每加侖 3.88 美元，創下兩年來的最高紀錄，這反映了原油成本上漲的直接傳導。為減緩衝擊，美國財政部宣布，該國政府正在考慮從戰略石油儲備中進行額外釋放，此前上週已授權歷史性的 4 億桶釋放。專家警告說，只要主要運輸路線仍然無法通行，這些措施可能只能提供暫時的緩解。

隨著衝突進入第三週，伊朗官員已表示，荷姆茲海峽的狀況不會恢復到戰前條件。據報導，德黑蘭的立法者正在起草一項法案，對未來任何通過該水道的商業交通徵收通行費和安全稅。經濟學家警告說，如果封鎖持續到本月底，持續的高價格可能引發 5000 億美元的全球經濟衝擊，可能使第四季度的全球增長減半。

timeline.title: US Intensifies Coastal Strikes as Global Oil Prices Sustain Triple-Digit Highs Amid Ongoing Hormuz Blockade
timeline.title_zh: 美軍加強沿海打擊，全球油價因霍爾木茲封鎖持續維持三位數高位
timeline.sub_title: American forces deployed bunker-buster munitions against Iranian coastal sites as oil prices breached 103 dollars, while the White House issued emergency waivers to mitigate a worsening domestic energy crisis.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 美軍對伊朗沿海地區部署「炸彈卡車」，油價突破103美元，同時白宮發布緊急豁免以緩解日益嚴重的國內能源危機
timeline.summary:
The global energy crisis reached a new fever pitch on Wednesday as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz entered its third week, driving Brent crude oil prices as high as 108 dollars per barrel. In response to the tightening chokehold on one-fifth of the world’s oil supply, the United States military intensified its offensive under Operation Epic Fury. U.S. Central Command confirmed that American forces utilized 5,000-pound bunker-buster munitions to target hardened Iranian missile sites and command-and-control facilities along the coastline. These strikes aim to neutralize the anti-ship cruise missiles and sea drones that have paralyzed commercial shipping, which has seen a staggering 97 percent decline in daily transits since the conflict began.

The military escalation has also moved into a phase of high-level targeted eliminations. Reports emerged on March 18 that the Minister of Intelligence of the Islamic Republic, Esmail Khatib, was killed in recent waves of strikes, following the confirmed death of Ali Larijani, the Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council. These leadership losses have prompted defiant rhetoric from Tehran. Iran’s new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, reiterated that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed as a tool of pressure, while state media issued chilling warnings of imminent strikes against oil and gas infrastructure in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the United Arab Emirates. This expansion of threats has raised fears that the conflict could soon engulf the entire Persian Gulf energy network.

On the water, the situation remains dire. Satellite imagery from March 18 captured the Egyptian-owned container ship SAFEEN PRESTIGE burning in the strait after a suspected second strike. At least [ref:3] 21 commercial vessels have been targeted since the onset of hostilities, forcing major shipping operators to anchor outside the chokepoint or reroute around the Cape of Good Hope. While most traffic has ceased, observers noted that Iran’s "Ghost Fleet" continues to move millions of barrels of crude eastward toward China, providing a critical financial lifeline for the regime even as it blocks other international tankers. This selective blockade has allowed Iran to continue generating an estimated 140 million dollars a day in oil revenue.

Politically, President Donald Trump has taken unprecedented steps to cushion the American economy from the shock. On Wednesday, the White House issued a 60-day waiver of the Jones Act, allowing foreign-flagged vessels to transport oil and gas between U.S. ports to lower domestic pump prices, which have spiked to a national average of 3.84 dollars per gallon. Despite these domestic measures, the effort to build an international maritime coalition has faced significant hurdles. NATO allies, including [ref:2, 1, 4] Germany and France, have expressed reluctance to join a formal escort mission, citing fears of becoming entangled in a full-scale regional war they did not initiate.

As the economic clock of war continues to tick, analysts at Goldman Sachs and other financial institutions warn that a prolonged closure could see oil prices peak as high as 150 dollars per barrel. With diplomatic channels nearly exhausted and Beijing remaining on the sidelines, the global community faces an uncertain wait to see if military force can successfully reopen the artery of the world's energy trade before the economic damage becomes irreversible and triggers a global recession.
timeline.summary_zh:
週三，隨著霍爾木茲海峽封鎖進入第三週，全球能源危機達到新的高潮，布倫特原油價格飆升至每桶108美元。為應對全球五分之一石油供應的緊縮，美國軍方加大了「史詩憤怒行動」的攻勢。美國中央司令部證實，美軍動用了5,000磅的「炸彈卡車」彈藥，襲擊了沿海地區的伊朗導彈陣地和指揮控制設施。這些打擊旨在摧毀癱瘓商業運輸的反艦巡航導彈和無人機，自衝突開始以來，商業運輸量已下降了驚人的97%。[ref:3]

軍事升級也進入了高級別定點清除階段。3月18日傳出消息，伊朗情報部長伊斯梅爾·哈提卜在近期的一系列襲擊中喪生，此前伊朗最高國家安全委員會秘書阿里·拉里賈尼的死亡已被確認。這些領導層的損失引發了德黑蘭方面的強硬言論。伊朗新任最高領袖穆吉塔巴·哈梅內伊重申，霍爾木茲海峽將繼續關閉作為施壓工具，而國家媒體則發出了對沙特阿拉伯、卡塔爾和阿拉伯聯合酋長國石油和天然氣設施即將發動襲擊的嚴厲警告。這些威脅的擴大引發了對衝突可能很快席捲整個波斯灣能源網絡的擔憂。

在海上，局勢依然嚴峻。3月18日的衛星圖像顯示，埃及擁有的貨櫃船「SAFEEN PRESTIGE」在海峽中燃燒，疑為第二次襲擊所致。自敵對行動開始以來，至少有21艘商船遭到襲擊，迫使主要航運公司停泊在海峽外或繞道好望角。儘管大部分交通已停止，但觀察人士注意到，伊朗的「幽靈艦隊」繼續向東運送數百萬桶原油運往中國，為該政權提供了關鍵的財政生命線，即使它封鎖了其他國際油輪。這種選擇性的封鎖使得伊朗能夠繼續產生估計每天1.4億美元的石油收入。[ref:3]

在政治層面，唐納德·特朗普總統採取了前所未有的措施來緩衝美國經濟免受衝擊。週三，白宮發布了為期60天的瓊斯法案豁免令，允許外國註冊船隻在美國港口之間運輸石油和天然氣，以降低國內汽油價格，美國全國平均價格已飆升至每加侖3.84美元。儘管採取了這些國內措施，但建立國際海上聯盟的努力面臨重大障礙。包括德國和法國在內的北約盟友已表示不願加入正式護航任務，理由是擔心捲入一場他們未發起的全面地區戰爭。[ref:2, 1, 4]

隨著戰爭經濟時鐘不斷滴答作響，高盛和其他金融機構的分析師警告稱，如果封鎖持續，油價可能飆升至每桶150美元。由於外交渠道幾乎枯竭，而北京仍袖手旁觀，國際社會將面臨一個不確定的等待，以確定軍事力量是否能在經濟損害不可逆轉並引發全球衰退之前成功重新開放世界能源貿易的動脈。[ref:null]

timeline.title: Global Oil Prices Sustain Highs as Major Allies Reject US Naval Coalition Demands
timeline.title_zh: 主要盟友拒絕美國海軍聯盟要求，全球油價維持高位
timeline.sub_title: Brent crude continues trading above one hundred dollars as European and Asian powers rebuff President Trump's call for a military task force to break the Iranian blockade.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 歐洲和亞洲國家拒絕特朗普總統組建軍事特遣部隊打破伊朗封鎖的呼籲，布倫特原油交易價繼續在每桶一百美元以上。
timeline.summary:
The global energy crisis triggered by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has entered a critical new phase today, with benchmark Brent crude prices stabilizing above $102 per barrel. This sustained price surge follows a period of intense market volatility as the conflict enters its third week. The standoff, which began after joint U.S. and Israeli military strikes in late February, has effectively paralyzed one of the world's most vital maritime chokepoints. [ref:7, 2, 1, 6] While oil prices saw a temporary dip earlier in the week on reports of limited vessel movements, they rebounded sharply on Tuesday as the geopolitical outlook dimmed and diplomatic solutions appeared increasingly remote.

The central development in the last 24 hours involves a widening diplomatic rift between the United States and its traditional allies. President Donald Trump issued a forceful demand for nations heavily dependent on Middle Eastern oil—including the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Japan, and South Korea—to join an international naval task force to reopen the waterway. However, the response has been overwhelmingly negative. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz explicitly ruled out military participation, characterizing NATO as a defense alliance rather than an interventionist one. Similarly [ref:5], officials from Italy and the European Union emphasized that the current crisis is not Europe's war, preferring instead to exhaust diplomatic channels. In response to these rebuffs, the U.S. President signaled a pivot toward unilateral action, stating that the United States does not require outside assistance to manage the situation.

On the water, the situation remains perilous and highly restricted. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi clarified Tehran's stance on Tuesday, asserting that the Strait remains open to most international shipping but strictly closed to vessels from enemy nations, specifically naming the United States, Israel, and their close military allies. Despite [ref:5] this rhetoric of a selective blockade, maritime tracking data suggests that commercial traffic has slowed to a trickle. Only 15 [ref:2, 5] vessels, including five tankers, were recorded transiting the passage over the last three days—a 90 percent decline compared to pre-crisis levels. This soft blockade is being enforced through intimidation and targeted strikes, such as the reported projectile hit on a tanker anchored near Fujairah today, which caused structural damage and prompted the United Arab Emirates to briefly close its airspace.

The economic consequences of the prolonged closure are cascading through global markets. Iraq, which relies on the Strait for the vast majority of its revenue, has begun desperate negotiations with Tehran to secure safe passage for its tankers to prevent a total domestic economic collapse. Meanwhile, major oil producers in the region, including Saudi Arabia and the UAE, have slashed production as storage facilities reach capacity and export routes remain blocked. Although the International Energy Agency has authorized the release of 400 million barrels of emergency reserves, analysts warn that this volume is insufficient to offset the daily loss of 20 million barrels normally transiting the Strait.

Looking ahead, financial institutions are raising their alarm levels. New forecasts from UBS and Standard Chartered suggest that if the blockade persists through April, Brent crude could easily surpass $150 per barrel. Beyond energy, the disruption is impacting the global supply of fertilizers, aluminum, and liquefied natural gas, threatening to trigger a significant inflationary spike across Western and Asian economies. With no clear military or diplomatic off-ramp in sight, the market premium is rapidly transforming from a temporary shock into a structural threat to the global economy.
timeline.summary_zh:
由霍爾木茲海峽封鎖引發的全球能源危機於今日進入關鍵新階段，基準布倫特原油價格穩定在每桶102美元以上。在衝突進入第三週、市場劇烈波動之後，油價持續上漲。這場對峙始於2月下旬美國和以色列的聯合軍事打擊，已有效癱瘓了世界上最關鍵的海上咽喉要道之一。雖然本週早些時候有報導稱船隻通行受限導致油價一度下跌，但隨著地緣政治前景黯淡、外交解決方案顯得愈發遙不可及，週二油價大幅反彈。[ref:7, 2, 1, 6]

過去24小時的核心發展是美國與其傳統盟友之間日益擴大的外交分歧。美國總統唐納·特朗普強烈要求依賴中東石油的國家——包括英國、法國、德國、日本和韓國——加入一個國際海軍特遣部隊，以重新開放水道。然而，回應絕大多數是負面的。德國總理弗里德里希·梅爾茨明確排除軍事參與，認為北約是一個防禦聯盟，而非干預性聯盟。同樣，意大利和歐洲聯盟的官員強調，當前危機不是歐洲的戰爭，並傾向於窮盡外交途徑。為回應這些拒絕，美國總統暗示將轉向單邊行動，表示美國無需外部協助來處理局勢。[ref:5]

海上局勢依然危險且受到嚴格限制。伊朗外交部長阿巴斯·阿拉格奇週二闡明了德黑蘭的立場，聲稱海峽對大多數國際航運保持開放，但嚴禁敵對國家的船隻通行，並點名美國、以色列及其緊密的軍事盟友。儘管有選擇性封鎖的言論，海事追蹤數據顯示商業航運已減至微乎其微。過去三天，僅記錄到15艘船隻（包括5艘油輪）通過該通道，與危機前水平相比下降了90%。這種軟性封鎖是通過恐嚇和定點打擊來實施的，例如今天報導的油輪在富查伊拉附近錨地遭受的砲擊，造成了結構損壞，並促使阿拉伯聯合酋長國短暫關閉了其領空。[ref:5, 2]

長期關閉造成的經濟後果正對全球市場產生連鎖反應。伊拉克，其大部分收入依賴於該海峽，已開始與德黑蘭進行絕望的談判，以確保其油輪的安全通行，防止國內經濟全面崩潰。與此同時，該地區的主要石油生產國，包括沙特阿拉伯和阿聯酋，由於儲存設施已滿且出口路線被阻斷，已大幅削減產量。儘管國際能源署已授權釋放4億桶應急儲備，但分析師警告，此數量不足以抵消通常通過該海峽的每日2000萬桶石油損失。[ref:null]

展望未來，金融機構正提高警報級別。瑞銀和渣打銀行的最新預測顯示，如果封鎖持續到4月份，布倫特原油價格很容易超過每桶150美元。除了能源，此次中斷還影響著化肥、鋁和液化天然氣的全球供應，威脅著引發西方和亞洲經濟體顯著的通貨膨脹。由於看不到明確的軍事或外交解決途徑，市場溢價正迅速從一次暫時性衝擊轉變為對全球經濟的結構性威脅。[ref:null]
